title: Knema oblongifolia
taxonomy: Knema oblongifolia is classified within the family Myristicaceae, a group of flowering plants commonly known as nutmegs or myristicas. This plant belongs to the genus Knema, which comprises a variety of species native to the tropical regions of Asia and Australia. The classification of Knema oblongifolia is further supported by its shared characteristics and morphological features with other members of the Myristicaceae family. As a member of the Myristicaceae family, Knema oblongifolia is an evergreen tree that produces small, white flowers and red or purple fruit. Its classification as a member of the Myristicaceae family is consistent across various botanical sources and taxonomic systems.

intro: Knema oblongifolia, commonly known as the Knema tree, is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Knema oblongifolia include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Knema oblongifolia is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
